NWS STORM SURVEY CONCLUDED THAT AN EF-1 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN WEST OF NEW BERN MONDAY EVENING WITH MAX WIND SPEEDS OF 80 TO 90 MPH. SEVERAL LARGE HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES WERE DAMAGED, DAMAGE TO CROPS WAS NOTED, AND NOTABLE DAMAGE OCCURRED TO A SINGLE FAMILY RESIDENCE.

TREE DAMAGE AND SIDING TORN OFF AN OUTBUILDING.

A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED A MICROBURST AS IT MOVED ACROSS STRABANE IN SOUTHWESTERN LENOIR COUNTY. THE INTENSE WINDS BEGAN IN AN OPEN FIELD JUST WEST OF SMITH-GRADY ROAD, AND THEN DAMAGED SEVERAL BUILDINGS AND TREES ACROSS THE ROAD. OF NOTE SEVERAL HARDWOOD TREES HAD LARGE PORTIONS OF THEIR CANOPY BROKEN OFF, INCLUDING ONE UPROOTED TREE. ADDITIONALLY, A TRUCK TRAILER WAS FLIPPED ON ITS SIDE AND A LARGE GARAGE OUTBUILDING WAS SHIFTED OFF ITS FOUNDATION BY THE WINDS. THE MICROBURST CONTINUED EASTWARD BLOWING LARGE LIMBS THROUGH THE ADJACENT FARM FIELD AND INTO THE STRAYBANE MOBILE HOME PARK. HERE NOT MUCH DAMAGE WAS SEEN OTHER THAN UNDERPINNING DAMAGE TO SEVERAL TRAILERS. FINALLY, ADDITIONAL HARDWOOD TREE DAMAGE WAS SEEN ABOUT A HALF MILE TO THE EAST JUST SOUTH OF NC 55. HERE A FEW LARGE (BUT WEAKENED) TREE LIMBS WERE BROKEN OFF IN A CONCENTRATED AREA.

A DAMAGE SURVEY FOUND EVIDENCE OF WIND SPEEDS UP TO 75 MPH DUE TO A THUNDERSTORM MICROBURST. SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE SNAPPED, AND A FEW TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN, INCLUDING ONE THAT FELL ON A PICKUP TRUCK. EXTENSIVE CROP DAMAGE WAS FOUND IN THE AREA AS WELL.
